baseline_for_comparison.read: Current Triton dot_k_atomic sparse dKV wall. It consumes materialized probs/grad_logits and atomically writes one KxD contribution per valid selected compressed group.

compile_failures.failure: Requested dynamic shared memory 206848 exceeds device limit 163840 for main_kernel
compile_failures.read: The matching vendored forward cannot launch at D512/H64 on MI350 without splitting or reducing the schedule.

compile_failures.failure: Requested dynamic shared memory 204800 exceeds device limit 163840 for sparse_mqa_bwd_kernel_kernel
compile_failures.read: The straight backward atomic reference cannot launch at the target H64/D512 shape.

compile_failures.failure: Requested dynamic shared memory 219104 exceeds device limit 163840 for sparse_mqa_bwd_kernel_kernel
compile_failures.read: Reducing selected block size did not fix shared-memory pressure; it made the generated schedule larger.

successful_timing_points.read: This already nearly equals the D512 Triton sparse-atomic wall while only covering half of D.
A simple two-way head split for H64/D512 was expected to land around 28.1 ms before integration overhead.
Concrete target-width head-split timing confirms the estimate: the straight atomic-add TileLang reference scales to about 28 ms at H64/D512, not to the 7.59 ms half-D point.

why: The vendored reference cannot compile at the target H64/D512 shape on MI350 because dynamic shared-memory demand exceeds the device limit.
The fitting split points are not fast enough: D256 S4096 is 7.59 ms for half-D, H32/D512 S4096 is 14.05 ms for half-heads, and the measured H64/D512 head-split path is 28.11 ms.
The concrete head-split target-width path is slower than the current 8.01 ms Triton sparse-atomic wall and far from the desired sparse-backward envelope.
decision.next: Use TileLang only as a rewrite surface if we can change the schedule: split Q/dO/KV staging to fit MI350 shared memory, preserve query-major MFMA contribution production, and add in-producer segmented/grouped dKV reduction before global atomics. Do not integrate the straight T.atomic_addx4 reference into TorchTitan.
